mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals labor market momentum that drives consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of top-quartile PAYEMS growth months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated consumer price inflation and a restrictive federal funds rate compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS fails to generate negative 60-day returns with Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals capacity contraction and an impending earnings recession, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansion periods, or Rank IC > -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rates above 4% indicate slackening labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC > -0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales and housing starts captures durable consumer demand momentum that lifts corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly high RSAFS and HOUST do not produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 102.5 signals manufacturing contraction and supply-side weakness that erodes corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO is below 102.5, or Rank IC > -0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that supports broad-based corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.
